title: Lifetime Value for Creator Businesses Quiz

questions.question: A creator runs a community membership at $97/month with an average retention of 8 months. What is the customer LTV, and how does the lesson say higher-priced memberships compare on retention?

options.text: $776 LTV; higher-priced memberships retain shorter because of the cost burden
$776 LTV; higher-priced memberships retain longer because members are more committed and see faster results
$970 LTV; retention is the same regardless of price point
$582 LTV; $97/month memberships typically retain for only 6 months

questions.explanation: LTV = $97 x 8 months = $776. The lesson makes the counterintuitive point that higher-priced memberships retain longer, not shorter. Members who pay $297/month are more committed, take the content more seriously, and see faster results -- all of which reduce churn.

questions.question: What LTV:CAC ratio range is considered the 'sweet spot' for most creator businesses?

options.text: 1:1 to 2:1 -- break-even is sufficient for creator businesses
3:1 to 5:1 -- healthy margins with room for growth investment
8:1 to 10:1 -- the higher the ratio, the better the business
10:1 or above -- creator businesses should have extremely low acquisition costs

questions.explanation: The sweet spot for most creator businesses is 3:1 to 5:1. Below 3:1, profit margins are too thin. Above 8:1, you are actually under-investing in growth and leaving revenue on the table. The lesson specifically notes that a ratio above 8:1 means you should be spending more on marketing.

questions.question: According to the membership churn benchmarks, what is the 'critical retention window' and what happens if a member survives it?

options.text: The first week; surviving it doubles retention
Months 1-3; surviving them increases the probability of staying 12+ months by 3x
The first 30 days; after which churn drops to near zero
Months 6-12; this is when most members decide whether to renew annually

questions.explanation: The critical retention window is months 1-3. Month 1 sees 10-20% churn, months 2-3 see 8-12% per month. But if a member survives the first 90 days, their probability of staying for 12+ months increases by 3x. This is why the best creator communities invest heavily in onboarding during the first 90 days.

questions.question: Calculate your current LTV using the formulas from this lesson (choose the formula that matches your offer type). Then estimate your CAC. What is your LTV:CAC ratio? Based on the benchmarks, is your business in a healthy range, and which of the 4 LTV levers (increase transaction value, increase purchase frequency, extend customer lifespan, build recurring revenue) would have the biggest impact on your ratio?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the student correctly applied the LTV formula for their offer type and provided a reasonable CAC estimate. Check that their LTV:CAC ratio interpretation aligns with the benchmarks (below 3:1 needs work, 3-5:1 is healthy, above 8:1 means under-investing in growth). Assess whether the LTV lever they chose is the most impactful for their specific situation.
